description: 22-question SAT Math timed-test module — practice version 2. Mirrors the digital-SAT Module 1 structure: 22 questions, 35 minutes, Desmos calculator allowed.

answerFormat.MC: options is array of 4 strings. correct is 0-indexed integer.
answerFormat.SPR: correctAnswer is the canonical string answer. UI should normalize student input.
answerFormat.diagram: Optional SVG string. Frontend should render above question text when present.

questions.question: A parking garage charges a flat fee of $3 plus $2 per hour. If Diego paid $15, for how many hours did he park?
questions.explanation: Set up 3 + 2h = 15. Subtract 3: 2h = 12. Divide: h = 6 hours.

questions.question: The function C(m) = 45 + 0.20m models the monthly cost C, in dollars, of a phone plan based on m, the number of minutes used. What does the value 0.20 represent in this context?
options: The total number of minutes included
The cost when no minutes are used
The flat monthly fee, in dollars
The cost per minute, in dollars
questions.explanation: In a linear function y = b + mx, the coefficient of the variable is the rate of change. Here, 0.20 is dollars per minute.

questions.question: If 2x + 3y = 18 and x - y = 4, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: From the second equation: x = y + 4. Substitute: 2(y+4) + 3y = 18, so 5y + 8 = 18, giving y = 2. Then x = 6.

questions.question: A student earns $12 per hour tutoring and needs to earn at least $180 this week. Which inequality represents the number of hours t the student must work?
questions.explanation: Earnings = 12t dollars. "At least $180" means earnings ≥ 180, so 12t ≥ 180.

questions.question: If (3x - 7)/(4) = 5, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Multiply both sides by 4: 3x - 7 = 20. Add 7: 3x = 27. Divide: x = 9.

questions.question: A water tank contains 240 gallons and drains at a constant rate of 8 gallons per minute. After how many minutes will the tank contain 80 gallons?
questions.explanation: Set up 240 - 8m = 80. Subtract 240: -8m = -160. Divide: m = 20 minutes.

questions.question: A grocery store sells apples for $1.50 each and oranges for $2 each. A shopper buys at least 8 pieces of fruit and spends no more than $20. If a represents the number of apples and o represents the number of oranges, which system of inequalities models this?
questions.explanation: "At least 8 pieces" → a + o ≥ 8. "No more than $20" with cost 1.5a + 2o → 1.5a + 2o ≤ 20.

questions.question: What is the smaller of the two solutions to x² - 7x + 10 = 0?
questions.explanation: Factor: (x-2)(x-5) = 0, so x = 2 or x = 5. The smaller is 2.

questions.question: A ball is thrown upward, and its height h, in feet, after t seconds is given by h(t) = -16t² + 64t + 5. What is the maximum height of the ball, in feet?
questions.explanation: For a parabola at² + bt + c with a < 0, the max occurs at t = -b/(2a) = -64/(-32) = 2. Substitute: h(2) = -16(4) + 64(2) + 5 = -64 + 128 + 5 = 69.

questions.question: A population of bacteria doubles every 3 hours. If the initial population is 500, what is the population after 12 hours?
questions.explanation: Number of doublings = 12/3 = 4. Population = 500 × 2⁴ = 500 × 16 = 8000.

questions.question: If f(x) = 3x² - 5, what is f(-2)?
Which expression is equivalent to (2x + 3)(x - 4)?
If √(x + 5) = 7, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Square both sides: x + 5 = 49. Subtract 5: x = 44.

questions.question: The function f(x) = (x - 3)² + 7 has its minimum value at what point?
questions.explanation: In vertex form f(x) = (x - h)² + k, the vertex (minimum, since the parabola opens up) is at (h, k) = (3, 7).

questions.question: A jacket that originally cost $80 is on sale for $60. What is the percent discount? (Enter your answer without the % symbol.)
questions.explanation: Discount = $80 - $60 = $20. Percent discount = 20/80 = 0.25 = 25%.

questions.question: A recipe calls for 3 cups of flour for every 2 cups of sugar. If a baker uses 12 cups of flour, how many cups of sugar are needed?
questions.explanation: Set up the proportion: 3/2 = 12/s. Cross-multiply: 3s = 24, so s = 8 cups.

questions.question: The test scores of 5 students are 72, 85, 90, 78, and 95. What is the mean (average) of these scores?
A bag contains 4 red marbles, 6 blue marbles, and 5 green marbles. If one marble is drawn at random, what is the probability it is NOT blue?
questions.explanation: Total marbles = 4 + 6 + 5 = 15. Not blue = 4 + 5 = 9. Probability = 9/15 = 3/5.

questions.question: A right triangle has legs of lengths 9 and 12. What is the length of the hypotenuse?

questions.skill: Circles — chord and perpendicular from center
questions.question: A chord of a circle is 16 units long. The perpendicular distance from the center of the circle to the chord is 6 units. What is the radius of the circle?
questions.explanation: Drop a perpendicular from the center to the chord; it bisects the chord, giving a right triangle with legs 8 (half the chord) and 6 (distance from center), and hypotenuse r (the radius). By Pythagorean: r² = 6² + 8² = 100, so r = 10.
questions.diagram:
<svg viewBox="0 0 300 280" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <!-- Circle (radius 100 px = 10 units; centered at (150, 120)) -->
  <circle cx="150" cy="120" r="100" fill="#FDF5EA" stroke="#2E2A25" stroke-width="2"/>

  <!-- Chord AB at y=180 (perpendicular distance 60px = 6 units below center) -->
  <line x1="70" y1="180" x2="230" y2="180" stroke="#C4602A" stroke-width="2.5"/>
  <circle cx="70" cy="180" r="3" fill="#2E2A25"/>
  <circle cx="230" cy="180" r="3" fill="#2E2A25"/>
  <text x="52" y="198" font-family="Georgia, serif" font-size="15" font-style="italic" fill="#2E2A25">A</text>
  <text x="237" y="198" font-family="Georgia, serif" font-size="15" font-style="italic" fill="#2E2A25">B</text>

  <!-- Perpendicular from O down to midpoint M of chord (dashed) -->
  <line x1="150" y1="120" x2="150" y2="180" stroke="#2E2A25" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3"/>
  <circle cx="150" cy="180" r="2.5" fill="#2E2A25"/>

  <!-- Right-angle marker where perpendicular meets chord -->
  <path d="M 140 180 L 140 170 L 150 170" fill="none" stroke="#2E2A25" stroke-width="1.5"/>

  <!-- Center O -->
  <circle cx="150" cy="120" r="3" fill="#2E2A25"/>
  <text x="156" y="116" font-family="Georgia, serif" font-size="15" font-style="italic" fill="#2E2A25">O</text>

  <!-- Length labels -->
  <text x="158" y="158" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="bold" fill="#2E2A25">6</text>
  <text x="142" y="205" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="bold" fill="#C4602A">16</text>
</svg>

questions.question: What is the distance between the points (1, 2) and (4, 6) in the coordinate plane?
In a right triangle whose two acute angles each measure 45°, the legs each have length 7. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: Since both acute angles measure 45°, this is an isosceles right triangle (the legs are equal). The hypotenuse equals leg × √2 = 7√2.
